dialogue_text: Help Dorothy to kill the Wicked Witch of the West,
source_excerpt: "Help Dorothy to kill the Wicked Witch of the West," replied the Beast. "When the Witch is dead, come to me, and I will then give you the biggest and kindest and most loving heart in all the Land of Oz."

dialogue_text: If he is a Beast when I go to see him, I shall roar my loudest, and so frighten him that he will grant all I ask. And if he is the lovely Lady, I shall pretend to spring upon her, and so compel her to do my bidding. And if he is the great Head, he will be at my mercy; for I will roll this head all about the room until he promises to give us what we desire. So be of good cheer, my friends, for all will yet be well.

dialogue_text: I am Oz, the Great and Terrible. Who are you, and why do you seek me?
source_excerpt: "I am Oz, the Great and Terrible. Who are you, and why do you seek me?"

speaker.notes: No canonical speaker could be inferred from the source context.

review_notes: Speaker could not be resolved from the source context.

dialogue_text: I am a Cowardly Lion, afraid of everything. I came to you to beg that you give me courage, so that in reality I may become the King of Beasts, as men call me.
source_excerpt: And the Lion answered, "I am a Cowardly Lion, afraid of everything. I came to you to beg that you give me courage, so that in reality I may become the King of Beasts, as men call me."

dialogue_text: Why should I give you courage?
source_excerpt: "Why should I give you courage?" demanded Oz.

speaker.notes: No canonical speaker could be inferred from the source context.

review_notes: Speaker could not be resolved from the source context.

dialogue_text: Because of all Wizards you are the greatest, and alone have power to grant my request,
source_excerpt: "Because of all Wizards you are the greatest, and alone have power to grant my request," answered the Lion.

dialogue_text: Bring me proof that the Wicked Witch is dead, and that moment I will give you courage. But as long as the Witch lives, you must remain a coward.
source_excerpt: The Ball of Fire burned fiercely for a time, and the voice said, "Bring me proof that the Wicked Witch is dead, and that moment I will give you courage. But as long as the Witch lives, you must remain a coward."

speaker.notes: No canonical speaker could be inferred from the source context.

source_excerpt: "What shall we do now?" asked Dorothy sadly.

dialogue_text: There is only one thing we can do,
source_excerpt: "There is only one thing we can do," returned the Lion, "and that is to go to the land of the Winkies, seek out the Wicked Witch, and destroy her."
